name: Modify GTT Order
summary: Modifies an existing GTT (Good Till Triggered) alert/order. Make a POST call to https://piconnect.flattrade.in/PiConnectAPI/ModifyGTTOrder with a jData JSON body (and jKey from login) to update the order parameters the alert will fire. On success it returns a JSON array with the alert id and a replacement status; on error it returns a JSON object with stat and emsg.

request_params.name: jData
request_params.description: Should send json object with fields in the jData list below (the modify request payload). Note: the Modify GTT Order section does not print an explicit REQUEST DETAILS parameter table; jData and jKey are shown only via the curl example body (jData={...}&jKey=...).

json_fields.name: uid
json_fields.description: User id of the logged in user.

json_fields.name: ai_t
json_fields.description: Alert Type, should be original alert type, can't be modified.

json_fields.name: d
json_fields.description: Data to be compared with LTP.

json_fields.name: remarks
json_fields.description: Any message Entered during order entry.

json_fields.name: trantype
json_fields.description: B -> BUY, S -> SELL [transtype should be 'B' or 'S' else reject].

json_fields.name: prctyp
json_fields.description: Price type / order type. (Doc has only a possible-value cell for this field; no description text.)

json_fields.name: ret
json_fields.description: Retention type [ret should be DAY / EOS / IOC else reject].

json_fields.name: qty
json_fields.description: Order Quantity [If qty is junk value other than numbers].

json_fields.name: prc
json_fields.description: Order Price [If prc is junk value other than numbers] "Order price cannot be zero".

json_fields.name: dscqty
json_fields.description: Disclosed quantity (Max 10% for NSE, and 50% for MCX) [If dscqty is junk value other than numbers].

response_fields.name: stat
response_fields.description: GTT order success or failure indication. (Success sample shows "Oi Replacedt"; failure sample shows "Not_Ok".)

response_fields.name: request_time
response_fields.description: This will be present only in a successful response.

response_fields.name: al_id
response_fields.description: Alert Id. (Returned in the sample JSON as "Al_id".)

response_fields.name: emsg
response_fields.description: This will be present only in case of errors. That is : 1) Invalid Input 2) Session Expired.

issues: The Modify GTT Order section (pages 31-33) does NOT print an explicit REQUEST DETAILS (jData/jKey) parameter table - unlike the sibling Cancel GTT Order section on page 33. jData/jKey are recorded as request_params but flagged as derived from the curl example body only. In the jData field table (page 32, verified against a zoomed crop) every field carries a trailing '*' (mandatory) EXCEPT al_id and d, which appear WITHOUT a '*' and are recorded required=false; al_id being optional is slightly surprising for a modify call. prctyp has only a possible-value cell (LMT / SL-LMT / DS / 2L / 3L) and no description text in the doc; the description supplied is a gloss, not verbatim. The success-response stat value reads "Oi Replacedt" verbatim (apparent typo for "Oi Replaced"). The response field is documented as al_id but appears as "Al_id" (capitalized) in the sample JSON. The curl --data body contains a "prevprd":"C" key that is NOT in the jData field table, and omits several documented fields (ai_t, al_id, remarks, d) - left verbatim. The page-31 right-column sample tail ("Al_id":"21041500000010") belongs to the PRECEDING Place GTT Order section, not Modify; Modify's own success Al_id is 21041500000008 (page 32). The curl uses placeholder host 'https://BaseURL/ModifyGTTOrder'; the real endpoint is https://piconnect.flattrade.in/PiConnectAPI/ModifyGTTOrder.
notes: Mandatory fields are marked with a trailing '*' in the doc; in the jData table all fields except al_id and d carry the asterisk. ai_t must be the ORIGINAL alert type and cannot be modified. trantype rejects anything other than 'B' or 'S'; ret rejects anything other than DAY/EOS/IOC. prc cannot be zero. dscqty (disclosed qty) max is 10% for NSE and 50% for MCX. jKey is the session key obtained on login. Success response is a JSON array; failure response is a JSON object. The section spans pages 31-33: page 31 left column carries the tail of the preceding Place GTT Order field/response table then the MODIFY GTT ORDER title + endpoint URL, while its right column carries the tail of the preceding sample ("Al_id":"21041500000010") + a failure response + the curl opening line; page 32 carries the full jData field table + RESPONSE DETAILS (left) and the curl --data body + both sample responses (right); page 33 carries only the tail of the emsg description ("2) Session Expired") before the next section (Cancel GTT Order). The PDF does not print a dedicated REQUEST DETAILS param table for Modify GTT Order.
